articles.title: Planning Permits for Rear and Side Extensions in 2024: Step-by-Step Guide
articles.content:
Extending your home usually triggers local planning and building rules. This 2024 guide walks through when you need a planning permit, when a building permit is enough, and how rear and side extensions are assessed.

We cover:
- How to check your zoning and overlays
- Typical rear extension setbacks and height limits
- How shadowing, overlooking and site coverage are assessed
- What documents you need for a planning permit application
- How long permits usually take and common reasons for delays

Planning permit vs building permit
A planning permit deals with how your extension affects the streetscape, neighbours and neighbourhood character. A building permit confirms that the proposed work complies with the Building Code and structural safety requirements.

When is a planning permit required for an extension?
You typically need a planning permit if:
- Your property is in a zone or overlay that controls development intensity or heritage
- You are increasing site coverage beyond local thresholds
- The rear extension exceeds height or setback limits

Working with a registered builder who understands planning permit extension requirements will minimise delays. A good builder will coordinate with your designer, prepare the documentation and communicate with council on your behalf.
articles.excerpt: Learn exactly when you need a planning permit for rear or side extensions in 2024, what councils look for, and how to avoid common delays.

articles.title: Do I Need a Planning Permit for a Rear Extension? (2020 Rules)
articles.content: This article explains the 2020-era rules for rear extensions, including when planning permits were required, how private open space and overlooking rules were applied, and what exemptions existed for small additions.
articles.excerpt: A look back at the 2020 requirements for planning permits on rear extensions.

articles.title: Designing a Kitchen Renovation on a $35,000 Budget
articles.content: A $35,000 budget can deliver a high-impact kitchen renovation when you prioritise layout, storage and lighting. This guide breaks down where to spend and where to save, including joinery choices, benchtop materials and appliance packages.
articles.excerpt: Practical tips for planning a stylish kitchen renovation on a $35k budget.

articles.title: Bathroom Waterproofing Basics Every Homeowner Should Know
articles.content: Waterproofing is one of the most critical parts of any bathroom renovation. In this article we explain Australian Standards, membrane types, fall to floor wastes, and what your warranty should cover if something goes wrong.
articles.excerpt: Understand how bathroom waterproofing works and what to expect from your builder.

articles.title: Loft Conversion Regulations: Head Height, Stairs and Fire Safety
articles.content: Loft conversions are a popular way to add bedrooms without extending the footprint of your home. This guide covers minimum head heights, stair rules, egress windows and fire-rated construction requirements.
articles.excerpt: Key regulatory considerations before committing to a loft conversion.

articles.title: Planning a Deck Extension for Outdoor Living
articles.content: A deck extension can create a seamless flow between your indoor living and the backyard. We outline structural requirements, timber vs composite choices, and when balustrades and permits are required.
articles.excerpt: How to plan a safe, durable deck extension that suits your lifestyle.

articles.title: The 5 Phases of a Full Home Renovation
articles.content: From initial briefing to handover, a full home renovation typically runs through five key phases: concept design, detailed documentation, approvals, construction and completion. We explain what happens at each stage and who is responsible for decisions.
articles.excerpt: Understand the full journey of a major renovation project from start to finish.

articles.title: 7 Cost-Saving Tips for Your Next Renovation
articles.content: Renovating on a budget doesn’t have to mean compromising on quality. This article shares seven practical ways to manage costs, from simplifying structural changes to choosing cost-effective finishes.
articles.excerpt: Smart ways to reduce renovation costs without cutting corners on quality.

articles.title: Do You Need a Planning Permit to Remove Internal Walls?
articles.content: Most internal wall changes relate to structural and building permits rather than planning permits, but there are exceptions. We explain when open-plan kitchen renovations can proceed without town planning approval and when overlays or heritage controls may trigger extra steps.
articles.excerpt: When kitchen and living room wall removals need more than just a building permit.

articles.title: Designing Energy-Efficient Extensions in Melbourne’s Climate
articles.content: Good orientation, insulation and glazing choices can dramatically improve comfort in your new extension. We discuss passive solar design principles, double glazing, and how to meet NatHERS energy rating targets.
articles.excerpt: How to make your new rear or side extension comfortable year-round.

articles.title: Bathroom Renovation Warranties Explained
articles.content: Bathroom renovations involve waterproofing, plumbing and electrical work, each of which may have different warranty periods. This guide explains statutory warranties, waterproofing guarantees and how to keep your warranty valid.
articles.excerpt: Understand what is covered by bathroom renovation warranties and for how long.

articles.title: Owner-Builder vs Registered Builder: Which Is Right for Your Extension?
articles.content: Thinking of managing your own extension as an owner-builder? We compare responsibilities, permits, insurances and risks so you can decide whether to engage a registered builder instead.
articles.excerpt: A side-by-side comparison of owner-builder and registered builder pathways for extensions.

faq_items.question: What warranty do you offer on premium bathroom renovations?
faq_items.answer:
All premium bathroom renovations come with a comprehensive warranty package. This includes:

- 10-year structural warranty on framing and substrates
- 7-year waterproofing warranty on membranes and wet area detailing
- 7-year warranty on workmanship
- Manufacturer warranties on fixtures and fittings as specified in your contract

Your warranty is valid provided that the bathroom is used and maintained in line with the care instructions we provide at handover. If you notice any defects or concerns, contact our team as soon as possible so we can assess and, where required, carry out warranty works.

faq_items.question: What warranty is included with kitchen renovations?
faq_items.answer: Our kitchen renovations include a 7-year workmanship warranty and structural warranty in line with Victorian regulations. Hardware such as hinges, runners and appliances are covered by manufacturer warranties, which we pass on to you at handover.

faq_items.question: Is structural work on extensions covered by warranty?
faq_items.answer: Yes. All structural work on extensions, including new footings, framing and roof structures, is covered by our 10-year structural warranty, subject to normal use and maintenance.

faq_items.question: How do you calculate renovation quotes?
faq_items.answer: We start with a site visit, measure-up and discussion of your goals and budget. Based on your scope, finishes and access, we prepare a detailed fixed-price quote. Provisional sums are only used where exact selections are not yet known.

faq_items.question: How long does a typical renovation take?
faq_items.answer: Timeframes vary with scope, but as a guide: bathroom renovations usually take 3–5 weeks, kitchen renovations 4–6 weeks, and medium-sized extensions 12–20 weeks from site start. Design and approvals are additional.

faq_items.question: Do I need a permit for a bathroom renovation?
faq_items.answer: Most bathroom renovations require a building permit if they involve structural changes, moving plumbing or altering waterproofed areas. We arrange building permits on your behalf and coordinate with the building surveyor.

faq_items.question: When is a building permit required for a kitchen renovation?
faq_items.answer: A building permit is generally required if you remove load-bearing walls, alter structural beams, or significantly change plumbing or gas lines. Cosmetic upgrades like new doors and benchtops usually do not require a permit.

faq_items.question: When do I need a planning permit for an extension?
faq_items.answer: You may need a planning permit for an extension if your property is in a zone or overlay that controls development, if you exceed site coverage limits, or if your design does not meet ResCode standards for overlooking and overshadowing. We review your property information and advise you before design proceeds too far.

faq_items.question: What does the typical payment schedule look like?
faq_items.answer: Payments are staged in line with domestic building contracts, usually including deposit, base, frame, lock-up and completion stages. Each claim is accompanied by a progress update and inspection opportunities.

faq_items.question: How do you manage site access and security during works?
faq_items.answer: We agree site access times with you before construction begins. Our team secures tools and materials and ensures temporary fencing or hoarding is used where required. You will have a single point of contact for any site access questions.
